serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Fried Eggs Sandwich (Bánh Mì Ốp La) is one sandwich, weighing approximately 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for similar components (baguette, fried egg, vegetables, condiments) to estimate for a typical Vietnamese Fried Eggs Sandwich.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Estimated based on white baguette (GI 70-75) as the primary carbohydrate component. Fried eggs and vegetables have minimal impact on overall GI.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from condiments and pickled vegetables.
hypertension.guidance: Limit portion size and request omission or reduction of high-sodium condiments like Maggi seasoning and soy sauce to manage blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index due to refined white baguette and significant carbohydrate load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Opt for wholemeal or multigrain bread if available, add more non-starchy vegetables, and control portion size to mitigate blood sugar spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium content and cholesterol from fried eggs.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Moderate intake due to sodium and cholesterol; consider requesting less oil for frying eggs and reducing high-sodium condiments to support cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Presence of nightshades (chilli) and high sodium.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals sensitive to nightshades should request omission of chilli. Reduce high-sodium condiments to help manage inflammation and fluid retention associated with RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Eggs Sandwich, known as Bánh Mì Ốp La in Vietnamese, is a popular street food featuring a crispy Vietnamese baguette filled with sunny-side-up fried eggs, fresh herbs like cilantro and mint, and often pickled carrots and daikon. The baguette is typically light and airy with a thin, crisp crust. The eggs provide a rich, savory flavor and creamy texture, complemented by the freshness of the herbs and the tangy crunch of the pickled vegetables. It is commonly seasoned with soy sauce and Maggi seasoning. Nutritionally, it is a significant source of carbohydrates from the baguette, protein and fat from the eggs, and can be high in sodium due to condiments. Its high glycemic index makes it a moderate concern for individuals managing blood sugar.
